summary: Frontend-only design-demo fully functional. Tested hero, header, services + BookingModal (calendar + slots + form + confirmation + error toasts), courses add-to-cart, cart drawer (qty/remove/subtotal), checkout (validation + Order Placed state + cart clear), footer newsletter input and journal section. All 12 primary acceptance criteria pass. No real bugs; two minor UX observations below.

issues: When user clicks the 'Courses' nav link or hero CTA 'Explore Courses', the section scrolls to the top and the first row of course cards (Akashic Records, Bhrigu Nandi Nadi, Lal Kitab) sit directly under the fixed translucent header. The header overlay visually (and semantically) covers ~80px of the first card row and can intercept clicks on the first 'Add' button until the user scrolls a bit further. Consider adding CSS `scroll-margin-top: 80px` (or an equivalent offset) on sections with id #services / #courses / #journal / #about / #contact so anchor-scrolls respect the fixed header height. The same applies to the #services section.

react-day-picker only shows the current month by default. Testing on 30 Apr 2026 resulted in only 3 selectable dates (30, 1 May, 2 May) because the next-month nav requires an extra click. Not a bug, but exposing the nav more prominently or defaulting to a two-month view would increase booking confidence near month-end.

action_items: Add `scroll-margin-top` (≈80–96px) to all section anchors (#about, #services, #courses, #journal, #contact) so fixed-header does not overlap the first row of content after anchor scrolls — improves click reliability on first cards.
(Optional) Default booking calendar to a 2-month view or add visible next-month affordance so users near end-of-month see more availability without extra interaction.
critical_code_review_comments: CartContext, BookingModal and CartDrawer are clean, concise, async-safe and use proper React idioms. No issues.
content.js `getAvailableSlots` unavailability logic is deterministic (day%7) — good for demo.

All interactive elements have proper kebab-case data-testid attributes (verified: hero-headline, hero-book-cta, hero-courses-cta, open-cart-button, cart-count-badge, nav-*, service-card-*, book-service-*, booking-modal, booking-calendar, time-slot-*, booking-input-name/email/phone, confirm-booking-button, booking-confirmation, booking-close-confirmation, course-card-*, add-to-cart-*, cart-drawer, cart-item-*, cart-increment-*/decrement-*/remove-*, cart-subtotal, checkout-button, checkout-input-*, place-order-button, order-placed-state, order-continue-button).
Hero uses an Unsplash cosmic-sky image (not from gitikasharma.in); acceptable for hero backdrop. All other service / course / journal / testimonial imagery correctly pulled from gitikasharma.in CDN.
Header is correctly transparent over hero and becomes glass-morphism after scroll (verified scroll listener).
test_credentials: N/A — purely mocked frontend, no auth
seed_data_creation: No seed data required. All mocks come from /app/frontend/src/data/content.js.
context_for_next_testing_agent:
The site is a single-page design demo with no backend. All cart/booking/order flows live in React Context + sonner toasts. Two flaky test-script pitfalls to remember: (1) the fixed header can intercept page.click() on first-row cards after scrollTo/anchor-scroll — always use `element.scroll_into_view_if_needed()` before clicking add-to-cart-akashic-records or book-service-kundali-analysis. (2) Calendar only shows current month by default; pick the latest enabled day (last in the :not([disabled]) list) to land on a weekday with partial slot availability. Acceptance criteria verified: hero headline present (split across
+ — match via data-testid='hero-headline' rather than full text), 6 service cards, booking modal with 7 slot buttons (some disabled/line-through), confirmation state, 6 course cards, cart-count-badge increments, drawer with subtotal/qty controls/remove, 3-stage drawer (cart→checkout→placed), order cleared after success, sonner error toasts on missing-fields. No console errors.
